features.title: Sunswift redesign foundation and Webflow text import

features.title: Opal-inspired light homepage, browser verification, and README
features.description: Update the Sunswift homepage toward the Opal Tadpole reference with a light image-led hero, Roobert-like typography, Tomorrow Today slogan, documented human workflows, and repeatable agent-browser verification.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB and S3, AWS build/test, frontend typecheck/lint
pnpm verify:browser: passed after installing Chrome for agent-browser; desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844 checked for content, dev overlays, screenshots, and horizontal overflow
HTTP smoke while dev server was running: /, /vehicles, and /admin/login returned 200

notes: agent-browser is now a local dev dependency with pnpm verify:browser.
Root README and app README document local running, testing, browser verification, and deploy testing.

features.title: Remove Opal naming, add transparent navbar and about section
features.description: Strip internal Opal references, rename the homepage hero, add a transparent overlay navbar with the Sunswift navigation, and extend the homepage with the Sunswift about copy and a placeholder image.
notes: Homepage hero renamed to HomepageHero and CSS hooks renamed to .homepage-hero-*.
Transparent navbar sits over the dark hero and links to all seven Sunswift sections.
About section uses placeholder garage.svg image until real Sunswift photography lands.

features.title: Typing slogan and Guinness records section
features.description: Add an Apple-style Guinness World Records section below the about block and animate the homepage slogan with a typing effect ending in 'Tomorrow, Today.'.
notes: Slogan now reveals one character at a time and ends with a blinking caret; an sr-only full slogan keeps accessibility intact.
Records section uses placeholder vehicle SVGs; swap with real photography when assets land.
Browser harness now waits for data-typing-complete before asserting the slogan and checks records copy.

features.title: Statement section, records kicker removed, vehicles full-page gallery
features.description: Drop the Guinness kicker, add an Opal-style big-text statement between hero and about, expand the vehicles dataset to all seven cars, and replace /vehicles with a hover-expand, click-to-open gallery that uses the whole page.
notes: Vehicles dataset now mirrors the Webflow source (Sunswift 7, VIolet, eVe, IVy, III, II, I).
Placeholder SVGs cover all seven cars; 3D renders are deferred until requested.
Vehicles detail view is client-state only — no /vehicles/[slug] route yet.

features.title: Monochrome theme with yellow accents and vehicles layout flip
features.description: Shift the site palette to black/white with a single Sunswift-yellow accent for important states (e.g. Join the team hover), flip the vehicles page so cards occupy the top and the headline anchors the bottom, and add staggered/blur transitions.
notes: Yellow exposed via --accent-yellow / Tailwind utilities (text-accent-yellow etc).
Vehicle gallery uses vehicle-card-rise, vehicle-fade-up, vehicle-detail-in keyframes with a reduced-motion guard.
Shared PageFrame chrome may still surface yellow via text-primary; a per-page accent audit is queued.

features.title: Remove statement section and darken zoom-reveal tone on scroll
features.description: Delete the HomepageStatement section, place the zoom-reveal on a light canvas, and tween the 'Built by Students. Driving Sustainability.' headline colour from a faint gray to near-black as the user scrolls, with the background vehicle render fading in alongside.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with the statement-removal + tone-shift assertions

notes: Headline tone driven by --zoom-text-color, interpolated rgb(186,186,186) -> rgb(12,12,12) via Math.pow(progress, 0.85).
Render slot now also fades in via --zoom-render-opacity (0.18 -> 0.65).
Reduced-motion fallback locks the headline to a readable dark color and the render to opacity 0.45.

features.title: Homepage zoom-reveal section with vehicle render placeholder
features.description: Add a new Opal Tadpole-style scroll section between the statement and about blocks that grows the 'Built by Students. Driving Sustainability.' headline toward the viewer while a vehicle render placeholder breathes in behind it.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with new HomepageZoomReveal mount, ordering, and scroll/Inter-Thin assertions

notes: Vehicle render uses the SR8 placeholder SVG; the data-homepage-vehicle-render slot is ready for a live 3D render or video element when the asset is ready.
Scroll behaviour is driven by --zoom-scale, --zoom-opacity, --zoom-tracking, --zoom-render-scale, and --zoom-render-y custom properties on the section root.
Reduced-motion users get the headline flat at its final scale/opacity, with no parallax on the render slot.

features.title: Top vignette, SR8 card, and abbreviated vehicle names

features.title: Vertical, thinner vehicle names on the gallery cards

features.title: Black-on-white primary with square liquid-glass surfaces

features.title: Cinematic achievements intro→timeline transition v2
features.description: Replace the subtle parallax intro transition with an unmistakable cinematic choreography. The intro section pins for ~140svh of scrolling and layers five effects: dramatic background zoom + darken, word-by-word headline scatter (translate + blur + fade), kicker/overview fade-up, a yellow accent wipe that sweeps left→right, a giant 1996 → 2026 year stamp that emerges centered, and a void overlay that hands off to the timeline.

evidence: node scripts/test-homepage-design.mjs: passed with new transition assertions (240svh stage, phaseScatter/phaseWipe/phaseHandoff, headline word spans, year stamp, accent wipe)
VERIFY_URL=http://localhost:3000 node scripts/verify-browser.mjs: passed with ACHIEVEMENTS_CONTRACT_OK:2023->2007

Manual agent-browser screenshot captures at introExit ≈ 0, 0.21, 0.5, and 0.75 confirmed the headline scatter, yellow wipe, and 1996 → 2026 year stamp render correctly at the expected scroll positions

notes: Intro section is height: 240svh with a sticky top-0 h-svh inner stage; introExit progress is computed from intro.getBoundingClientRect().top vs (offsetHeight - innerHeight).
Three named phase variables drive choreography: phaseScatter (0.18→0.6), phaseWipe (0.55→0.85), phaseHandoff (0.78→1.0).
Headline split into per-word inline-block spans with whitespace-pre so word boundaries are preserved for body.innerText (the browser verifier matches on the full phrase).
All motion effects guarded by prefers-reduced-motion: reduce media query, leaving the static page text + image in view for accessibility users.

The records list now uses editorial rows instead of the previous three-card block.
